--- engine: knitr --- # Assembly functional annotation {#funcanno}
![](/images/pathways.png){style="width:200px; background:white; border-radius:5px"}
In this section we will carry out carry out functional annotation of our MAGs/bin/bins. There are many different ways to carry this. In this tutorial we will use 2 tools: - [Bakta](#bakta_chap): Annotation of Bacterial genomes and plasmids - [MinPath](#minpath_chap): Prediction of metabolic pathways - Include MetaCyc and KEGG pathways ## Mamba
![](/images/mamba_logo.png){style="width:200px; border-radius:5px"}
Go back to your shotgun_meta terminal (or create a new one and use `. useshotgun`). ## Taxonomic annotation {-}
![](/images/classification.png){style="width:150px; background:white; border-radius:5px"}
Taxonomic annotation of bins can be carried out with `Kraken2`. As we have already done this for the reads and taxonomic results from read and assembly approaches have similar performances we will not cover it here. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html} ``` ::: :::
